title: carex salina subsp. spiculosa
intro: Carex salina subsp. spiculosa, also known as saltwort sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a plant that grows in coastal areas, particularly in salt marshes and sand dunes, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Carex salina subsp. spiculosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
